matches.x: CHAPTER XXX. Job’s honour is turned into contempt, his prosperity into calamity. UT. now they that are younger than I have me in derision, whose fathers I would have disdained to have set with the dogs of my flock.
In the present, though, who mocks him? (30:1) - Young men whose fathers Job had disdained to put even with the dogs of his flock

• The woman fleeing to the wilderness is the Church during the great apostasy.

CHAPTER XXX. Job’s honour is turned into contempt, his prosperity into calamity. UT. now they that are younger than I have me in derision, whose fathers I would have disdained to have set with the dogs of my flock.
‘What help and profit have we from those idols which thou dost worship, And before which thou dost bow thyself?

In summarizing his plight, what sort of things does he say? (30:26-31) - I looked for good, evil came to me; I waited for light, then came darkness - My heart is in turmoil and cannot rest; days of affliction confront me - I go about mourning, I cry for help - My skin grows black and falls from me; my bones burn with fever
